title: Using workload identity to push images to GCR
body: Can skopeo use workload identity to authenticate to GCR while pushing images from inside a container?

comments.body: Yes, it can via an credential helper. I use google-credential-gcr inside my Kubernetes cluster running on Google Kubernetes Engine. https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/docker-credential-gcr
Hi guys!  I did not find any documentation about this.  It'd be nice to add docs about how to get skopeo to use the credential helper before closing the issue at the very least.
Reopening to make sure we get the documentation piece covered.  Thanks for the poke @yashbhutwala !

> Hi guys! I did not find any documentation about this. It'd be nice to add docs about how to get skopeo to use the credential helper before closing the issue at the very least.

AFAICT, skopeo uses credential helpers just like the docker client. Run "docker-credential-gcr configure-docker" to generate a configuration in "~/.docker/config.json". Everything specific to Google Container Registry is covered in the docker-credential-gcr documentation.

Using credhelpers is documented in `man containers-auth.json`.  I don't think there's any documentation work left to do.

@TomSweeneyRedHat I let you decide :)
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
